items.title: Govt warns firms against 'channel switching' fuel abuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan has issued a stern warning to corporations and industrial players against "channel switching", where subsidised retail fuel is exploited to avoid paying market rates. He said some companies had been discreetly purchasing diesel at the RM6.72 per litre retail pump price instead of the RM7 to RM8 per litre industrial rate, effectively taking advantage of government-absorbed subsidies. Amir Hamzah said enforcement efforts were being stepped up at petrol stations and national borders to curb smuggling and ensure domestic fuel supply remains stable.

items.title: Sunway International Schools host Open Day with experiential classes, tours
items.summary: SUNWAY International Schools (SIS) is hosting an Open Day on April 18, offering parents and students a chance to experience its international education programmes firsthand. The event will be held at both Sunway International School Kuala Lumpur (SISKL) and Sunway International School Iskandar Puteri (SIS Iskandar). Sunway International Schools Open Day Date: April 18 (Saturday) Time: 10am to 3pm TWO CAMPUSES, ONE WORLD-CLASS EDUCATION SIS offers world-class education from kindergarten to pre-university, providing three internationally recognised pathways: Canadian, Cambridge and International Baccalaureate (IB).

items.title: PM Anwar: Malaysia at turning point to shift from smart cities to AI cities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysia is now at a critical juncture to transition from Smart City development to Artificial Intelligence (AI) City development as the foundation for its aspiration to become an AI Nation, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said this was emphasised during a special meeting he chaired this morning at the Prime Minister’s Office to review and coordinate the implementation of an AI City model for Malaysia. Previously, Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said Malaysia is actively implementing the National AI Action Plan to achieve its aspiration of becoming an AI Nation by 2030.

items.title: Sack Rafizi? Anwar says focus on economy problem more important now
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today brushed aside questions over Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli’s position in the party, saying attention should instead be focused on managing the economy. Anwar, who is also prime minister and finance minister, was asked to respond to Rafizi’s challenge for PKR to sack him following a second show-cause letter over alleged breaches of party disciplinary and ethical rules. This morning Rafizi said he had received a second show-cause letter from disciplinary board chairman Datuk Zainol Samah, who alleged the Pandan MP had repeatedly made negative statements against the party and Anwar.

items.title: Langkawi ferry operator raises fares for non-MyKad holders by RM10 amid rising costs
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 10 — Langkawi Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd has announced a RM10 fare increase for non-MyKad passengers, effective April 15, 2026. After the adjustment, non-MyKad passenger fares from Kuala Perlis to Langkawi will be RM45 one way for adults and RM35 for children. Fares for Malaysian citizens remain unchanged at RM21 (adult) and RM16 (child) from Kuala Perlis, and RM26.50 (adult) and RM20.50 (child) from Kuala Kedah.

items.title: Paddy farmers seek stronger government support as costs rise, food security at risk
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 10 — Pertubuhan Persaudaraan Pesawah Malaysia (PeSAWAH), an organisation that represents and advocates paddy farmers, has called for stronger and sustained government support to help the group cope with rising production costs and ensure the sustainability of the country’s rice industry. Its chairman, Abdul Rashid Yob, said the increase in fuel prices has significantly impacted farm operating costs, necessitating a comprehensive approach to ensure the sector remains sustainable. Therefore, urgent government intervention is vital to support farmers and ensure the sustainability of the paddy industry,” he told reporters after a meeting session with farmers from Perlis, Penang and Perak here today.

items.title: Vietnamese man pleads guilty to possessing python bile and tongues
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU: A Vietnamese man pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court here today to two charges of possessing more than 1,000 python parts without a licence, involving a protected wildlife species. He was charged with possessing 1,022 python bile and 191 python tongue parts from the Python reticulatus species at a premises in Taman Johor Jaya at about 9.50pm on April 4. Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) prosecuting officer Mas Izzaty Lokman objected to bail, citing the seriousness of the offence and the large quantity of seized items, which suggested an organised activity.

items.title: Artemis astronauts to shed light on space health risks
items.summary: WASHINGTON: While the Artemis II astronauts have been protected from the icy vacuum of space on their journey, their bodies have nonetheless been left exposed to possibly high levels of radiation – a danger of space travel that Nasa is anxiously waiting to study. Their trip around the Moon has taken the four astronauts farther into space than any human before – more than 1,000 times the distance from Earth to the International Space Station. The US space agency installed radiation sensors on the Orion capsule and took blood samples of the astronauts before takeoff to compare with samples post-trip.

items.title: Federal Court to hear PKR appeal over Zuraida’s RM100,000 bond ruling on July 16
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 10 — The Federal Court has fixed July 16 to hear Parti Keadilan Rakyat’s (PKR) appeal against a Court of Appeal decision that reduced former Ampang MP Datuk Zuraida Kamaruddin's bond payment from RM10 million to RM100,000 in a party bond breach case. On Tuesday, the Federal Court dismissed Zuraida's application to review its earlier decision granting PKR leave to appeal the appellate court's ruling. Earlier, on June 23, 2023, the Kuala Lumpur High Court ruled in favour of PKR and ordered Zuraida to pay RM10 million in accordance with the bond's terms.

items.title: Police cripple gang behind armed robberies in Penang
items.summary: A team from the Penang police contingent headquarters Criminal Investigation Department carried out coordinated operations at several locations in Chemor, Perak, and Seberang Prai Tengah, Penang. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said police also seized items believed to belong to victims, including jewellery, mobile phones and cash amounting to RM2,770. "We believe the group is linked to five gang robbery cases — one in Seberang Prai Utara, two in Seberang Prai Tengah and two in Seberang Prai Selatan," he added.

items.title: EPF: Akaun Fleksibel withdrawals largely for short-term survival, but gap seen across income groups
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Malaysians tapping into their Employees Provident Fund (EPF) savings under the Akaun Fleksibel facility are largely using the money to cover daily necessities, healthcare and debt repayments rather than discretionary spending, according to the pension fund’s latest report. About 93 per cent used funds for daily necessities, 81.7 per cent for emergency-related expenses, 74.4 per cent for debt and investment obligations, and 73.3 per cent for seasonal or lifestyle costs. Health treatments accounted for 58.7 per cent, while debt payments represented 56.6 per cent.

items.title: MetMalaysia warns of thunderstorms across KL, Putrajaya and 8 states until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as eight states, until 7pm today. The same warning has also been issued for Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim), Pahang (Cameron Highlands and Jerantut) and Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang). In Negri Sembilan, the affected areas are Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah and Rembau.

items.title: Mobile messaging jumps 240pct as chat-based commerce rises in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is seeing a faster-than-average shift towards conversational commerce, with total platform interactions rising 17 per cent in 2025, according to Infobip's Messaging Trends Report 2026. Mobile messaging app interactions surged 240 per cent during the year, while WhatsApp usage rose 40 per cent and Viber increased 107 per cent. Voice and video interactions grew 38 per cent, and email rose 20 per cent, signalling rising demand for multi-channel communication strategies.

items.title: Penang ambulance crash fuels debate over road safety at junctions
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: An ambulance collided with a car at a busy traffic light junction near Universiti Sains Malaysia in Penang, sparking debate over road safety. The video has since sparked discussion online, with many highlighting the risks faced by emergency vehicles at busy intersections. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Explainer: Five things to know about the US-Iran talks in Islamabad
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: The United States and Iran are holding their highest-level talks in years in Islamabad in a Pakistan-brokered bid to turn a fragile two-week ceasefire into a lasting end to a war that has roiled global energy markets. On April 8, the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan. Iran's Supreme National Security Council said the talks could continue for up to 15 days.

items.title: MRT Corp to begin land acquisition for second segment of Penang LRT Mutiara Line
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 10 — MRT Corp will start the land acquisition process for the second segment of the Penang LRT Mutiara Line that runs between Macallum on the island and Penang Sentral in Butterworth, said Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook. He said a total of 38 private lands have been identified for acquisition. “A total of 61 privately owned land lots have completed the land hearing process under the Land Acquisition Act 1960,” he said.

items.title: Porsche deliveries tumble further in first quarter
items.summary: Global deliveries were down 15 per cent in the first quarter at 60,991 vehicles, the German sports car maker said on Friday. Porsche reported a 10 per cent drop in deliveries to North America, driven partly by the discontinuation of US tax incentives for electric vehicles, according to a company statement. Deliveries in the rest of Europe plunged 18 per cent.

items.title: Cheras karaoke brawl: Magistrate fines 34 men after guilty pleas in gang-linked attack
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The Magistrates’ Court here today fined 34 men after they pled guilty to taking part in a violent riot at an entertainment centre in Cheras last week, an incident that involved firearms, explosives and other weapons. Two others, aged 19, were each fined RM2,000 in default three months’ jail. The court heard that the group was involved in a violent incident at a karaoke centre in Cheras at about 7.30pm on April 5, where they were found in possession of firearms, bullets, explosives, and weapons including sticks and stones that could be used to cause injury.

items.title: Toddler rescued after leg gets stuck in bathroom floor trap
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A two-year-old boy was rescued by firefighters after his leg became trapped in a bathroom floor trap at an apartment in Bandar Sri Damansara today. The Selangor Fire and Rescue Department said it received an emergency call at 12.13pm, prompting the deployment of a team from the Sungai Buloh Fire and Rescue Station. Its assistant director of operations, Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar, said the incident involved a toddler whose leg was lodged in a pipe drain beneath the bathroom floor trap of a unit at Pangsapuri Sri Meranti.

items.title: Dubai caps foreign flights until May 31, Indian carriers hardest hit
items.summary: NEW DELHI: Dubai has restricted foreign airlines to just one daily flight to its airports until May 31 due to the Iran crisis, raising concerns over revenue losses among Indian carriers, which had planned more flights than airlines from any other country, according to letters seen by Reuters. The Federation of Indian Airlines (FIA), which represents major carriers IndiGo, Air India and SpiceJet, has urged the Indian government to press Dubai authorities to lift the curbs. In a March 27 email to airlines, seen by Reuters, Dubai Airports said carriers would be limited to one return flight per day to Dubai International Airport (DXB) — typically the world's busiest international hub — and Al Maktoum International Airport (DWC) during the summer season from April 20 to May 31.

items.title: Trump puts Vance in the hot seat to lead peace talks, end Iran war
items.summary: WASHINGTON: It was a war JD Vance never wanted. Now the US vice president has been tasked with trying to end it. Vance is heading to Pakistan this week under orders from President Donald Trump to turn a fragile Iran ceasefire into a lasting peace agreement.

items.title: Langkawi ferry fares up RM10 for non-MyKad passengers
items.summary: After the adjustment, non-MyKad passenger fares from Kuala Perlis to Langkawi will be RM45 one way for adults and RM35 for children. From Kuala Kedah, fares will be RM55 for adults and RM42 for children. Fares for Malaysian citizens remain unchanged at RM21 (adults) and RM16 (children) from Kuala Perlis, and RM26.50 (adults) and RM20.50 (children) from Kuala Kedah.

items.title: Anwar: Go easy on fuel as global energy shock drives up subsidy bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today urged Malaysians to use fuel more prudently as global energy pressures intensify and place growing strain on the government’s RON95 subsidy bill. He said the administration is trying to maintain the subsidised price of RON95 for now, even as rising international oil prices significantly increase fiscal costs. Anwar’s remarks come amid concerns over global energy market volatility, with the government previously assuring that current fuel stock levels remain sufficient in the near term.

items.title: GOF arrests woman for selling LPG cylinders without permit
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The General Operations Force (GOF) has arrested a woman for selling liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinders without approval from the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry on Tuesday. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the 58-year-old woman was detained at Kampung Baguh in Rantau Panjang. The seized items comprised 10 Petronas LPG cylinders (14kg), three Petron 12kg cylinders and two BHP 12kg cylinders, with a total estimated value of RM1,899.

items.title: Malaysia to shift from Smart Cities to AI Cities, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is set to shift from Smart City development towards AI Cities as part of efforts to accelerate its ambition of becoming an artificial intelligence-driven nation, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a Facebook post, Anwar said he chaired a special meeting to review and coordinate the implementation of the AI City model for Malaysia, stressing that the country was now at a critical juncture in its digital transformation journey. "This effort demands a strong ecosystem, including secure AI and data governance, high-capacity digital infrastructure, local talent development, as well as policy and legislative frameworks that support responsible innovation," he said.

items.title: Sungai Buloh escape: Prison Dept details breach during custody transfer, confirms joint hunt with polic
items.summary: SUNGAI BULOH, April 10 — The Prisons Department today confirmed that a remand detainee escaped from custody during a transfer process following court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court, prompting a nationwide manhunt. Police earlier identified the escapee as Muhammad Hassan, 24, a Pakistani remand detainee facing multiple serious charges, including sexual offences involving a child, robbery-related offences, and immigration violations. “The Malaysian Prisons Department has confirmed that a person accused escaped from police custody after the conclusion of court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court.

items.title: Batang Kali chicken farm ordered to close over fly infestation complaints
items.summary: HULU SELANGOR: A chicken farm in Batang Kali has been ordered to close for 14 days after complaints of fly infestation affecting nearby residents. The Hulu Selangor Municipal Council (MPHS) said the action was taken by the Environmental Control Unit, Solid Waste Management and Public Cleansing Corporation under By-Law 43 of the Trade, Business and Industries By-Laws. "It is also required to submit a fly control plan and chicken waste management plan for evaluation," it said in a statement.

items.title: Detainee escapes during transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison; manhunt on
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Pakistani detainee, earlier reported to have fled custody, escaped during a transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison after attending court proceedings at the Shah Alam Court last night. "The Prisons Department confirms that the detainee managed to escape from custody after the completion of trial proceedings at the Shah Alam Court," it said in a statement today. It was earlier reported that police had launched a manhunt for a remand detainee who escaped from Sungai Buloh Prison yesterday.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC widens probe into RM300mil share transaction scandal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has widened its probe into the RM300 million share transaction scandal, focusing on suspected overseas fund movements and questionable valuation practices linked to the deal. The latest development comes a day after a former chief executive officer of a government-linked statutory body and a holding company chairman were remanded over their alleged roles in the transaction. "Investigations indicate the valuation used in the transaction was unreasonable and disproportionate, which could have resulted in losses exceeding RM300 million in public funds.

items.title: Farmer pleads guilty to slamming infant daughter, threatening to kill father
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: "All this was caused by 'mushroom' (a vape liquid laced with synthetic drugs)," said a farmer after pleading guilty at the Sessions Court and magistrate's court here today to charges of injuring his infant daughter and threatening to kill his father. The accused, Muhammad Shaffik Amat Zairudin, 29, who has one child, entered guilty pleas before judge Osman Affendi Mohd Shalleh and magistrate Arun Noval Dass. At the magistrate's court, he pleaded guilty to two counts of criminal intimidation under Section 506 of the Penal Code for threatening to kill his 62-year-old father.

items.title: 99pct backing for Penang Mutiara LRT signals strong public confidence – Loke
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Nearly 99 per cent of respondents have expressed support for the Penang Mutiara Line Light Rail Transit (LRT) project, reflecting strong public backing for the state's flagship transport initiative. The encouraging response follows public inspection sessions for the proposed alignment linking Komtar and Penang Sentral, which have drawn more than 16,000 feedback submissions to date. Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook said the strong show of support demonstrates clear public confidence in the project and its long-term benefits.

items.title: [UPDATED] Malaysian vessel clears Strait of Hormuz, oil supply remains stable, says PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian-owned vessel has successfully cleared the Strait of Hormuz and is now on its way home, with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim offering cautious reassurance that the country's oil supply for April and May remains secure. Anwar said he was briefed on the latest developments by Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) president and group chief executive officer Tan Sri Tengku Muhammad Taufik Tengku Aziz earlier in the day. Anwar expressed appreciation to Iran for facilitating the transit of Malaysian oil tankers through the strategic waterway, describing the move as important for safeguarding the country's energy security.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Ministries study ways to ease impact of diesel price hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Several ministries are studying ways to ease the burden on sectors affected by rising diesel prices due to the ongoing Middle East crisis, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said assistance had already been provided to farmers, smallholders and fishermen, while efforts were being made to support other affected sectors. On RON95 fuel, the prime minister said the government remained committed to maintaining the price despite the financial pressure on public finances.

items.title: Anwar: One Malaysia-bound oil tanker breaks down in Hormuz, another has docked; no petrol supply impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — One oil tanker carrying Malaysian-bound crude from the Persian Gulf has broken down, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. In March, Anwar said he spoke with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian about Malaysia’s “non-hostile” status and neutral foreign policy to secure an agreement for seven Malaysian-flagged vessels safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Roughly 38 per cent of Malaysia’s crude oil imports pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: APAD revokes licence of transport firm in Gemas trailer crash that killed three
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) has revoked the operator licence of MFG Barokah Enterprise, effective immediately, following the company’s involvement in a fatal trailer accident in Gemas last week that killed three people. In a statement, APAD said its investigation revealed the company had failed to comply with multiple licence conditions and safety requirements. The agency’s Licence Suspension and Revocation Committee met on April 9 and determined that MFG Barokah Enterprise had failed to adhere to the Industry Code of Practice (ICOP), ensure proper vehicle maintenance, and monitor its driver’s behaviour.

items.title: Residents near Sungai Buloh Prison on edge as manhunt continues for escaped detainee
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Residents near Sungai Buloh Prison are on edge following the escape of a remand prisoner, with many tightening security, keeping a close watch on their surroundings and staying alert for suspicious activity. Defani Savaer, 39, who has lived in Bandar Seri Coalfields for nine years, said the incident has altered her sense of security. Concern quickly spread among residents.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Singapore's stance on Strait of Hormuz is its prerogative
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Singapore's decision not to negotiate with Iran over safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz is its own prerogative, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the republic was entitled to its position, while stressing that Malaysia had adopted a clear and firm stance on the matter. "We cannot discuss Iran's response (on closing the strait) without also addressing the injustice — the barbaric Israeli attacks on Iran, backed by the United States.

items.title: EPF: Nearly two-thirds of members haven’t used Akaun Fleksibel despite RM16.6b withdrawals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Some 63 per cent of Employees Provident Fund (EPF) members eligible for withdrawals under the Akaun Fleksibel facility have yet to tap into it, despite nearly RM16.6 billion already being withdrawn by others as of October 2025. Under the revised structure, contributions are split into three accounts: 75 per cent into Akaun Persaraan, 15 per cent into Akaun Sejahtera, and 10 per cent into Akaun Fleksibel. During the initial transfer period, which ended on August 31, 2024, some 4.1 million members moved RM14.5 billion into Akaun Fleksibel.

items.title: Axiata unit secures US$50mil from Maybank subsidiary for Cambodian digital push
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Cambodia has extended a US$50 million financing to Axiata Group Bhd's unit Smart Axiata to support the expansion of Cambodia's digital and telecommunications infrastructure including 5G connectivity. Maybank Cambodia and Smart Axiata said they will continue to deepen their collaboration to advance Cambodia's connectivity-driven development and support broader Asean regional progress. Maybank Cambodia chief executive officer and country head Rath Sophoan said the financing facilitates community development and the digital economy in line with Cambodia's Digital Economy and Society Policy Framework 2021 to 2035 and Maybank's ROAR30 sustainability and new economy priorities.

items.title: [UPDATED] 34 men fined for rioting at Cheras entertainment outlet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thirty-four men involved in a violent attack at an entertainment outlet in Cheras were fined between RM2,000 and RM3,000 by the magistrate's court today. Thirty-two of the accused were fined RM3,000 each, in default four months' imprisonment. They are Tan Chee Chin, G. Bathmanathan, M. Kogilan, Lim Puay Fei, Kevin Yong, Ong Zhi Jian, Looi Khai Xuan, Voison Chor, Tan Jun Ning, Hee Tuck Choy, Ching Thong Hai, Kee Jin Xuan, Lim Teong Yow, Chan Kuang Man and Ho Chee Seong.

items.title: Four in 10 Malaysian room rentals across peninsula exclude at least one race, Architects of Diversity survey shows
items.summary: In the study titled The Room Rental Discrimination Vol. 2: The Semenanjung Report, AOD found that 43.6 per cent of 40,294 listings on rental platform iBilik included explicit racial preferences, making exclusion more common than listings open to all races. Overall, only 22.6 per cent of listings were open to all races, while 33.8 per cent did not state any preference.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Focus on economy, not Rafizi row
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged calm over growing calls within PKR for action to be taken against Pandan member of parliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli. Anwar, who is also party president, dismissed the internal party row as a personal matter unworthy of attention amid more pressing concerns. On Tuesday, PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said calls for action against Rafizi would be discussed by the disciplinary board.

items.title: Seventeen-year-old pleads not guilty to sexually assaulting boy, 10, at Batu Caves
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — A resident of a children’s welfare home pleaded not guilty at the Selayang Magistrate’s Court today to four charges of committing physical and non-physical sexual assault, as well as unnatural intercourse, against a boy. For the second charge, the teenager is accused of committing physical sexual assault against the same victim at the same place, time and date under Section 14(b) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, punishable under Section 14 of the same Act, which provides for a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping. However, the owner of the welfare home, S. Valan, pleaded not guilty to four charges of physical sexual assault.

items.title: Azalina: Second meeting on separating A-G, prosecutor roles examined various views
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 (Bernama) – Efforts to separate the roles of Attorney General (AG) and Public Prosecutor are being strengthened through engagement sessions with multiple parties, including non-governmental organisations and civil society. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform), Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, said at its second meeting yesterday, Parliament's Special Select Committee examined various views, particularly on the appointment, termination and accountability of the Public Prosecutor. "Yesterday I chaired the second meeting of the Special Select Committee on the separation of the A-G and Public Prosecutor's roles to advance institutional reform towards greater transparency and integrity," she said in a Facebook post.

items.title: Malaysia's March palm oil stocks hit seven-month low as exports surge
items.summary: Malaysia's palm oil stocks tumbled 16.1 per cent to 2.27 million metric tons in March from February, marking their lowest level since August, data from the Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) showed on Friday. Crude palm oil production increased 7.2 per cent to 1.38 million tons, snapping a four-month run of declines. Palm oil exports rocketed 40.7 per cent higher to 1.55 million tons, the most in five months.

items.title: Lease idle farmland to boost food security, says agriculture minister
items.summary: LUNDU, April 10 — Owners of idle or underutilised agricultural land are urged to consider leasing their land to ensure it remains productive and continues contributing to the nation’s food supply. Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the initiative would help optimise land use while reducing Malaysia’s reliance on food imports. “Typically, companies seeking to lease land require long-term arrangements of 10 years or more, as agriculture takes time from planting to yield,” he said after visiting the Tanjung Purun padi planting project today.

items.title: Azalina: Second meeting on AG-prosecutor split examines key views
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Efforts to separate the roles of Attorney General (AG) and Public Prosecutor are being strengthened through engagement sessions with multiple parties, including non-governmental organisations and civil society. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, said at its second meeting yesterday, Parliament’s Special Select Committee examined various views, particularly on the appointment, termination and accountability of the Public Prosecutor “Yesterday I chaired the second meeting of the Special Select Committee on the separation of the AG and Public Prosecutor’s roles to advance institutional reform towards greater transparency and integrity,” she said in a Facebook post. She said engagement sessions will continue to ensure all views are considered in efforts to strengthen the nation’s justice system.

items.title: RM36m wildlife bust: Vietnamese man admits to possession of 1,000 python bile, 191 tongues after Johor Jaya raid
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 10 — A 39-year-old Vietnamese man pled guilty in the Sessions Court here today to possessing protected wildlife parts, including 1,022 python bile and 191 python tongues. The wildlife parts are listed as protected species under the First Schedule of the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716). Authorities previously reported that Perhilitan seized wildlife parts worth RM36.8 million in a raid at the same Taman Johor Jaya premises.

items.title: Zahid: Umno among Asean’s oldest parties as it turns 80
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said Umno, which is approaching its 80th anniversary, remains one of the oldest political parties in the Southeast Asian (Asean) region, with a legacy of struggle that reflects resilience and a key role in national development. The Deputy Prime Minister said that throughout its long history, Umno has undergone various phases of struggle and shifts in the political landscape, reflecting its experience, resilience and key role in national development. Ahmad Zahid, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, posted this in conjunction with Umno’s 80th anniversary.

items.title: Babysitter, mother claim trial to abusing four-year-old girl in Kuala Lumpur
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — A babysitter and her mother pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here yesterday to a charge of abusing a four-year-old girl that resulted in physical injuries. A. Saranya, 28, and R. Saroja, 64, were jointly charged, as persons having care of the child, with abusing the victim, resulting in injuries to her thigh and genital area at their residence in Jalan Pudu Impian here between February 2026 and April 3, 2026. Deputy public prosecutor Ravindejit Kaur suggested bail be set at RM30,000 each with one surety, however, lawyer Mohammad Arifin Abdul Wahab, representing the two accused, asked for a lower bail amount, saying Saranya is nine months pregnant and due to deliver her sixth child this month, while her mother, who is unemployed, suffers from hypertension and high cholesterol.

items.title: Johor religious authorities expected to issue fatwa on TikTok user’s teachings after investigation, says exco
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 10 — The Johor Mufti’s Department is expected to issue a fatwa regarding the teachings of a TikTok user who is being reviewed for alleged deviancy. Johor Islamic Religious Affairs Committee chairman Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid said that the Johor Islamic Religious Affairs Department (JAINJ) is currently conducting an investigation and research into the beliefs of the woman who uses the social media handle Ratu Nur Putri Ratu Balqis. Mohd Fared said that the woman’s social media posts, which disseminated her beliefs, have been taken down.

items.title: Dzulkefly: Malaysia’s medicine supply stable until June despite rising costs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 10 — The country’s medicine supply remains stable and sufficient at least until the end of June, thanks to combined government and industry stockpiles. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad said the government is preparing in case the West Asia conflict persists, including implementing medium- and long-term mitigation measures. He said supply chain disruptions, including delays in medical device shipments since before March, have also pushed up prices.

items.title: Anwar mourns death of ‘exceptional diplomat’ former Iranian foreign minister Kharazi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today expressed deep sorrow over the passing of former Iranian Foreign Minister Dr Kamal Kharazi, describing him as a man of rare intellect and genuine warmth, always thoughtful and gracious. He said Kharazi's untimely death resulted from wounds inflicted by Zionist terrorist attacks. “I first came to know Dr Kharazi during his years as Iran’s envoy to the United Nations in New York, and our friendship carried through his distinguished tenure as Foreign Minister.

items.title: Eleven-state e-waste crackdown nets RM582m haul, 515 arrests in major bust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Authorities have carried out a major nationwide crackdown on illegal e-waste activities under Op Hazard 3.0, with hundreds arrested and large-scale seizures recorded across multiple states. Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the operation involved coordinated enforcement across 11 states and 110 targeted locations. Mohd Khalid said 2,147 police officers and personnel from multiple enforcement agencies were deployed throughout the operation.

items.title: Malaysian Bar moves to challenge AG’s NFA decision in Zahid corruption case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — The Malaysian Bar is seeking a judicial review to quash the attorney-general’s (AG) decision not to continue criminal proceedings against Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Bar is also seeking a mandamus order compelling the AG to resume prosecution or reinstate charges against Zahid under Section 254A of the CPC. According to the news report, the Bar asserted that the discontinuance contradicts the High Court’s earlier finding that the prosecution had established a prima facie case against Zahid.

items.title: RTM sign language interpreter Abby Tan dies a month after road accident, minister extends condolences
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has extended his condolences to the family of Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) sign language interpreter Mohammad Fadhil Tan Razman Tan, who died today. He said Mohammad Fadhil, better known as Abby Tan, had been involved in a road accident on March 6. Fahmi also expressed his condolences to Mohammad Fadhil’s colleagues at RTM, especially in the News and Current Affairs Department, and called on the public to pray that his soul be blessed and placed among the righteous.

items.title: Over 5,000 litres of subsidised diesel seized in Perak raid over alleged profiteering scheme
items.summary: IPOH, April 10 — The Perak Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) seized 5,070 litres of subsidised diesel in a raid at Kampung Raja Hitam near Ayer Tawar early today. He said the raided timber and hardware premises were believed to have been used as a transit point to illegally accumulate diesel supplies before redistributing them to the industrial market. “A man was found transferring liquid believed to be diesel from a three-tonne lorry into an intermediate bulk container (IBC).

items.title: No proof MACC involved in ‘corporate mafia’ case, says IGP as probe continues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 10 — Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail today said there is no evidence connecting the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) to any “corporate mafia” activity as alleged. But he said investigations are ongoing and several people have been identified for questioning, both in Malaysia and overseas. He said cases involving foreign jurisdictions will require government-to-government cooperation as well as agency-to-agency.

items.title: Kuala Pilah man attacks brother with machete after dispute
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 10 — A man was injured after being attacked with a machete by his older brother in a scuffle at a house in Taman Air Mawang, Johol, here yesterday. Kuala Pilah district police chief Superintendent Muhamad Mustafah Hussin said police received a report on the incident involving the 27-year-old victim at 3.49pm. “The victim’s brother later came to the house with a machete and attacked him.

items.title: Sarawak trials edible rabies vaccines to boost immunisation for free-roaming dogs
items.summary: KUCHING, April 10 — Sarawak has begun distributing oral rabies vaccine (ORV) bait to free-roaming dogs as part of efforts to strengthen rabies control measures in the state. During the ORV briefing and training programme held near the Padawan Municipal Council (MPP) here, Department of Veterinary Services Sarawak (DVSS) deputy director Dr Nicholas Jenek said the programme involves giving edible vaccine bait to dogs that are difficult to catch or handle. Dr Ad Vos delivers a briefing on the oral rabies vaccine (ORV) programme during the session.
